Maharashtra Weather Forecast: Light Rain Likely in Mumbai; IMD Issues Red Alert for Heatwave in Several Regions
By Lokmat Times Desk | Updated: May 23, 2026 18:53 IST2026-05-23T18:53:12+5:302026-05-23T18:53:20+5:30

The India Meteorological Department (IMD) on Saturday, May 23, said conditions remain favourable for the further advance of the southwest monsoon over parts of the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal and the Andaman Sea during the next three to four days. According to the Regional Meteorological Centre in Mumbai, the southwest monsoon has already advanced into some parts of the southwest and southeast Arabian Sea, and most parts of the Andaman Sea as of May 23.
IMD also issued a red alert for the heatwave in several districts of Maharashtra for the next 2 to 3 days. Heatwave to severe heatwave likely at many places of Akola, Amaravati, Wardha, and Yavatmal. People in these regions would feel warm nights.
Thunderstorm accompanied with lightning, light to moderate rain, gusty winds with speed 40-50 kmph very likely to occur at a isolated places in the districts of Konkan- Goa and Madhya Maharashtra and Marathwada.
— Regional Meteorological Center,Mumbai (@RMC_Mumbai) May 23, 2026
Mumbai Weather Update
Mumbai and neighbouring districts are likely to witness light rain and thundershowers over the next 48 hours. The IMD forecast a partly cloudy sky with the possibility of light rain in the city and suburbs, with maximum temperatures expected to hover around 34 to 35 degrees Celsius and minimum temperatures around 28 degrees Celsius.
The weather department has also issued thunderstorm warnings for several districts in Maharashtra, including Thane, Raigad, Pune, Satara, Kolhapur and parts of Marathwada. Gusty winds of 30 to 50 kmph along with lightning and light to moderate rainfall are likely at isolated places over the next two days.
In Mumbai, light rain or thundershowers are likely on May 23 and 24, while dry weather conditions are expected from May 25 onwards. Thane district may witness thunderstorms accompanied by lightning, gusty winds and moderate rainfall at isolated places on Saturday.
Raigad and Ratnagiri districts are likely to receive moderate rainfall with thunderstorms and gusty winds reaching up to 50 kmph. Similar weather conditions have also been forecast for Pune and the ghat regions over the next two days.

The IMD said isolated rainfall activity was reported in parts of Konkan, Goa and Madhya Maharashtra on Friday. Pune district recorded some of the highest rainfall in the state, with Chinchwad receiving 6 cm rainfall.
Strong winds were also recorded in several areas, including Talegaon in Pune district at 61 kmph and Narayangaon at 54 kmph.
Meanwhile, Vidarbha continues to remain under severe heatwave conditions. The IMD has issued heatwave to severe heatwave warnings for districts including Akola, Amravati, Chandrapur, Nagpur, Wardha and Yavatmal over the next five days. Warm night conditions are also likely at isolated places in the region.
The highest maximum temperature in the region was recorded at Surendranagar in Gujarat at 43.8 degrees Celsius. The weather department has advised people to avoid open areas during thunderstorms, stay away from tall trees and electrical conductors, and follow local traffic advisories in case of heavy rain or strong winds.
The IMD also issued advisories for farmers, urging them to complete harvesting of mature crops, avoid chemical spraying during rainfall activity, and ensure proper storage of harvested produce.
For fishermen, the IMD warned of squally weather conditions over the Kerala and Karnataka coasts, Lakshadweep and adjoining areas of the Arabian Sea until May 27. Wind speeds of 40 to 60 kmph are expected in these regions, and fishermen have been advised not to venture into the sea during the warning period.
